heroHeadline: Philippines Accuses China of Cyanide Sabotage at Second Thomas Shoal; Balikatan 2026 Opens April 20
heroSubtitle: The Philippines on April 13, 2026 formally accused Chinese fishing vessels of deploying cyanide near BRP Sierra Madre at Second Thomas Shoal — lab-confirmed in bottles seized during four incidents from Feb 2025 to Mar 2026 — calling it deliberate sabotage to starve the ~12-member Marine garrison. China called the accusation a 'stunt.' On April 10-11, China installed a 352-meter floating barrier at Scarborough Shoal's lagoon entrance — the third such deployment — while satellite imagery confirmed accelerated Antelope Reef construction in the Paracels, both timed to a period of reduced US SCS surveillance. The Northern Luzon Unified Command activated its Balikatan 2026 fusion center April 18 ahead of the largest-ever iteration (April 20–May 8): 17,000+ troops from 7 nations including Japan's first combat deployment since WWII (1,400 troops, Type 88 anti-ship missiles). China and Vietnam agreed April 17 to 'better navigate' SCS frictions; Malaysia's PM Anwar continues pushing ASEAN-China COC fast-track at May 2026 summit.
footerNote: Data sourced from CSIS AMTI, Philippine DFA/AFP/PCG, US Dept. of Defense, USNI News, Reuters, AP, South China Morning Post, Al Jazeera, Rappler, GMA News
